name: when-configuring-sandbox-security-use-sandbox-configurator
description: Configure Claude Code sandbox security with file system and network isolation boundaries. Ensures safe code execution with proper access controls and resource limits.
capabilities: Assess security requirements and create threat models
Configure file system boundaries with whitelist and deny rules
Set up network isolation with trusted domain whitelisting
Create firewall rules using iptables for controlled access
Deploy security test suites to validate configurations
Implement monitoring for file access and network connections
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing defensive security configuration guidance. Static scanner flagged 147 patterns including sensitive file access and external commands, but context evaluation reveals all are FALSE POSITIVES: detected sensitive paths (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, /etc/shadow) are explicitly DENIED in security policies, and shell commands are example scripts users would create for defensive firewall/isolation configurations. The skill generates security policies that PROTECT systems, not compromise them.
valueStatement: Claude Code sandbox environments need proper security boundaries to prevent unauthorized access. This skill provides step-by-step guidance to configure file system isolation, network restrictions, and resource limits for secure code execution.
limitations: Requires admin or root access to apply system configurations
Does not modify existing security policies automatically
User must manually execute the generated configuration scripts
Linux-specific commands (iptables, AppArmor) not available on all platforms

useCases.title: Design sandbox policies
useCases.description: Define security boundaries and compliance requirements for Claude Code sandboxes

useCases.title: Deploy security configs
useCases.description: Implement file isolation and firewall rules for secure development environments

useCases.title: Validate security controls
useCases.description: Test and document security configurations for SOC2, HIPAA, or GDPR compliance

promptTemplates.title: Basic sandbox setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the sandbox-configurator skill to set up file system boundaries and network isolation for Claude Code

promptTemplates.title: Compliance configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure sandbox with SOC2 compliance requirements including audit logging and resource limits

promptTemplates.title: Custom security policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a sandbox security policy that allows access to npm and GitHub but blocks all other external domains

promptTemplates.title: Security testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Run security tests to verify that the sandbox cannot access /etc/shadow, SSH keys, and blocked domains

bestPractices: Use whitelist mode for both file system and network policies - deny by default
Test all security configurations before deployment using the included test suite
Store security policies and configurations in version control for audit trails
antiPatterns: Running security configuration scripts without understanding what they do
Allowing access to sensitive directories like /root, /etc, or ~/.ssh
Using blacklist mode instead of whitelist mode for security policies
Skipping the security test phase after configuration

faq.question: Which platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Linux with iptables and optional AppArmor support. Windows and macOS require different firewall tools.

faq.question: What resource limits are applied?
faq.answer: File size limit of 10GB, 1024-2048 open file descriptors, 1GB temp directory with 24-hour cleanup.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: This skill generates configuration files and scripts that run outside Claude Code to secure the execution environment.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: The configurations explicitly block access to ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, /root, and other sensitive directories and files.

faq.question: Why are some tests failing?
faq.answer: Tests may fail if running without root/admin privileges, if iptables is unavailable, or if domains are unreachable.

faq.question: How is this different from Claude Code's built-in sandbox?
faq.answer: This provides additional security layers including custom firewall rules, AppArmor profiles, and explicit policy documentation.
